revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 多爾夜神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|49|33.5|N|138|46|22.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]安良里321 |位置 = {{Coord|34|49|33.5|N|138|46|22.6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[積羽八重事代主命]] |社格 = [[伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = }} '''多爾夜神社'''（たにやじんじゃ）<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]安良里（あらり）にある[[神社]]。[[伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|伊豆国の式内社]]の1つである。
== 概要 == 創建不詳だが、[[式内社]]なので『[[延喜式神名帳]]』が作られた[[延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - [[コトバンク]]</ref>。
社名は「谷家」が語源とされ、同地区の山道を上がった先にある山神社が元宮とされる。
11月3日の例大祭では、同地区の出崎神社と共に、赤装束の子供たちによる猿舞（猿っ子踊り）が披露される<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf 伊豆地域郷土芸能] - ACTJT</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[積羽八重事代主命]]
== 境内 == * 忠魂碑 * 忘れじの碑 - 元[[予科練]]生（海軍少年飛行兵）が、当時の住民の親切に感謝して[[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）に建立<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto 忘れじの碑] - 西伊豆観光協会</ref>。
== 猿舞（猿っ子踊り）== 由来は諸説あるが、漁に関係した祈願が込められていることは共通している<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。演目は、 # 立ち踊り # 扇踊り # 逆立ち踊り # 鯨突き踊り の4つ<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。
元来は青年が1人で踊っていたが、地区の青年団が無くなって後、[[1980年]]（昭和55年）に踊りが復活してからは、小学生が集団で演じるようになった<ref name="act-jt"/>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:たにやしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:西伊豆町]]

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

revision.comment: 5 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 多爾夜神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|49|33.5|N|138|46|22.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]安良里321 |位置 = {{Coord|34|49|33.5|N|138|46|22.6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[積羽八重事代主命]] |社格 = [[伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=多爾夜神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|49|33.5|N|138|46|22.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''多爾夜神社'''（たにやじんじゃ）<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]安良里（あらり）にある[[神社]]。[[伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|伊豆国の式内社]]の1つである。
== 概要 == 創建不詳だが、[[式内社]]なので『[[延喜式神名帳]]』が作られた[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - [[コトバンク]]</ref>。
社名は「谷家」が語源とされ、同地区の山道を上がった先にある山神社が元宮とされる。
11月3日の例大祭では、同地区の出崎神社と共に、赤装束の子供たちによる猿舞（猿っ子踊り）が披露される<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf 伊豆地域郷土芸能] - ACTJT</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[積羽八重事代主命]]
== 境内 == * 忠魂碑 * 忘れじの碑 - 元[[予科練]]生（海軍少年飛行兵）が、当時の住民の親切に感謝して[[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）に建立<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto 忘れじの碑] - 西伊豆観光協会</ref>。
== 猿舞（猿っ子踊り）== 由来は諸説あるが、漁に関係した祈願が込められていることは共通している<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。演目は、 # 立ち踊り # 扇踊り # 逆立ち踊り # 鯨突き踊り の4つ<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。
元来は青年が1人で踊っていたが、地区の青年団が無くなって後、[[1980年]]（昭和55年）に踊りが復活してからは、小学生が集団で演じるようになった<ref name="act-jt"/>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:たにやしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:西伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:西伊豆町の歴史]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[多爾夜神社]] to [[Taniya Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Merging Japanese Wikipedia import with English content

== 祭神 == * [[積羽八重事代主命]]
== 境内 == * 忠魂碑 * 忘れじの碑 - 元[[予科練]]生（海軍少年飛行兵）が、当時の住民の親切に感謝して[[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）に建立<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto 忘れじの碑] - 西伊豆観光協会</ref>。
== 猿舞（猿っ子踊り）== 由来は諸説あるが、漁に関係した祈願が込められていることは共通している<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。演目は、 # 立ち踊り # 扇踊り # 逆立ち踊り # 鯨突き踊り の4つ<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。
元来は青年が1人で踊っていたが、地区の青年団が無くなって後、[[1980年]]（昭和55年）に踊りが復活してからは、小学生が集団で演じるようになった<ref name="act-jt"/>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:たにやしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:西伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:西伊豆町の歴史]]

== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 多爾夜神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|49|33.5|N|138|46|22.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]安良里321 |位置 = {{Coord|34|49|33.5|N|138|46|22.6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[積羽八重事代主命]] |社格 = [[伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=多爾夜神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|49|33.5|N|138|46|22.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''多爾夜神社'''（たにやじんじゃ）<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]安良里（あらり）にある[[神社]]。[[伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|伊豆国の式内社]]の1つである。
== 概要 == 創建不詳だが、[[式内社]]なので『[[延喜式神名帳]]』が作られた[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - [[コトバンク]]</ref>。
社名は「谷家」が語源とされ、同地区の山道を上がった先にある山神社が元宮とされる。
11月3日の例大祭では、同地区の出崎神社と共に、赤装束の子供たちによる猿舞（猿っ子踊り）が披露される<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf 伊豆地域郷土芸能] - ACTJT</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[積羽八重事代主命]]
== 境内 == * 忠魂碑 * 忘れじの碑 - 元[[予科練]]生（海軍少年飛行兵）が、当時の住民の親切に感謝して[[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）に建立<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto 忘れじの碑] - 西伊豆観光協会</ref>。
== 猿舞（猿っ子踊り）== 由来は諸説あるが、漁に関係した祈願が込められていることは共通している<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。演目は、 # 立ち踊り # 扇踊り # 逆立ち踊り # 鯨突き踊り の4つ<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。
元来は青年が1人で踊っていたが、地区の青年団が無くなって後、[[1980年]]（昭和55年）に踊りが復活してからは、小学生が集団で演じるようになった<ref name="act-jt"/>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:たにやしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:西伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:西伊豆町の歴史]]
==End Japanese==

[[JA:多爾夜神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q117097275}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
{{translated page|ja|多爾夜神社|version=103426790|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 多爾夜神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|49|33.5|N|138|46|22.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]安良里321 |位置 = {{Coord|34|49|33.5|N|138|46|22.6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[積羽八重事代主命]] |社格 = [[伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=多爾夜神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|49|33.5|N|138|46|22.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''多爾夜神社'''（たにやじんじゃ）<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]安良里（あらり）にある[[神社]]。[[伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|伊豆国の式内社]]の1つである。
== 概要 == 創建不詳だが、[[式内社]]なので『[[延喜式神名帳]]』が作られた[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - [[コトバンク]]</ref>。
社名は「谷家」が語源とされ、同地区の山道を上がった先にある山神社が元宮とされる。
11月3日の例大祭では、同地区の出崎神社と共に、赤装束の子供たちによる猿舞（猿っ子踊り）が披露される<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf 伊豆地域郷土芸能] - ACTJT</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[積羽八重事代主命]]
== 境内 == * 忠魂碑 * 忘れじの碑 - 元[[予科練]]生（海軍少年飛行兵）が、当時の住民の親切に感謝して[[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）に建立<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto 忘れじの碑] - 西伊豆観光協会</ref>。
== 猿舞（猿っ子踊り）== 由来は諸説あるが、漁に関係した祈願が込められていることは共通している<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。演目は、 # 立ち踊り # 扇踊り # 逆立ち踊り # 鯨突き踊り の4つ<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。
元来は青年が1人で踊っていたが、地区の青年団が無くなって後、[[1980年]]（昭和55年）に踊りが復活してからは、小学生が集団で演じるようになった<ref name="act-jt"/>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
[[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:西伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:西伊豆町の歴史]]
==End Japanese==

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== 境内 == * 忠魂碑 * 忘れじの碑 - 元[[予科練]]生（海軍少年飛行兵）が、当時の住民の親切に感謝して[[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）に建立<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto 忘れじの碑] - 西伊豆観光協会</ref>。

元来は青年が1人で踊っていたが、地区の青年団が無くなって後、[[1980年]]（昭和55年）に踊りが復活してからは、小学生が集団で演じるようになった<ref name="act-jt"/>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
==End Japanese== [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:西伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:西伊豆町の歴史]]

== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 多爾夜神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|49|33.5|N|138|46|22.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]安良里321 |位置 = {{Coord|34|49|33.5|N|138|46|22.6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[積羽八重事代主命]] |社格 = [[伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=多爾夜神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|49|33.5|N|138|46|22.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''多爾夜神社'''（たにやじんじゃ）<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]安良里（あらり）にある[[神社]]。[[伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|伊豆国の式内社]]の1つである。
== 概要 == 創建不詳だが、[[式内社]]なので『[[延喜式神名帳]]』が作られた[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - [[コトバンク]]</ref>。
社名は「谷家」が語源とされ、同地区の山道を上がった先にある山神社が元宮とされる。
11月3日の例大祭では、同地区の出崎神社と共に、赤装束の子供たちによる猿舞（猿っ子踊り）が披露される<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf 伊豆地域郷土芸能] - ACTJT</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[積羽八重事代主命]]
== 境内 == * 忠魂碑 * 忘れじの碑 - 元[[予科練]]生（海軍少年飛行兵）が、当時の住民の親切に感謝して[[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）に建立<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto 忘れじの碑] - 西伊豆観光協会</ref>。
== 猿舞（猿っ子踊り）== 由来は諸説あるが、漁に関係した祈願が込められていることは共通している<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。演目は、 # 立ち踊り # 扇踊り # 逆立ち踊り # 鯨突き踊り の4つ<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。
元来は青年が1人で踊っていたが、地区の青年団が無くなって後、[[1980年]]（昭和55年）に踊りが復活してからは、小学生が集団で演じるようになった<ref name="act-jt"/>。

[[JA:多爾夜神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q117097275}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
{{translated page|ja|多爾夜神社|version=103426790|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 多爾夜神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|49|33.5|N|138|46|22.6|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|西伊豆町|Q1203949}}安良里321 |位置 = {{Coord|34|49|33.5|N|138|46|22.6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|積羽八重事代主命}} |社格 = {{jalink|伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|Q11380552|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|村社}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=多爾夜神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|49|33.5|N|138|46|22.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''多爾夜神社'''（たにやじんじゃ）<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|西伊豆町|Q1203949}}安良里（あらり）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|Q11380552|伊豆国の式内社}}の1つである。
== 概要 == 創建不詳だが、{{jalink|式内社}}なので『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』が作られた{{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{jalink|コトバンク|Q11302260}}</ref>。
社名は「谷家」が語源とされ、同地区の山道を上がった先にある山神社が元宮とされる。
11月3日の例大祭では、同地区の出崎神社と共に、赤装束の子供たちによる猿舞（猿っ子踊り）が披露される<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf 伊豆地域郷土芸能] - ACTJT</ref>。
== 祭神 == * {{jalink|積羽八重事代主命}}
== 境内 == * 忠魂碑 * 忘れじの碑 - 元{{jalink|予科練}}生（海軍少年飛行兵）が、当時の住民の親切に感謝して{{jalink|1975年|Q2479}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}50年）に建立<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto 忘れじの碑] - 西伊豆観光協会</ref>。
== 猿舞（猿っ子踊り）== 由来は諸説あるが、漁に関係した祈願が込められていることは共通している<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。演目は、 # 立ち踊り # 扇踊り # 逆立ち踊り # 鯨突き踊り の4つ<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。
元来は青年が1人で踊っていたが、地区の青年団が無くなって後、{{jalink|1980年|Q2439}}（昭和55年）に踊りが復活してからは、小学生が集団で演じるようになった<ref name="act-jt"/>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
==End Japanese== [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:西伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:西伊豆町の歴史]]

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''多爾夜神社'''（たにやじんじゃ）<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|西伊豆町|Q1203949}}安良里（あらり）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|Q11380552|伊豆国の式内社}}の1つである。

=== 概要 ===
創建不詳だが、{{jalink|式内社}}なので『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』が作られた{{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{jalink|コトバンク|Q11302260}}</ref>。

社名は「谷家」が語源とされ、同地区の山道を上がった先にある山神社が元宮とされる。

11月3日の例大祭では、同地区の出崎神社と共に、赤装束の子供たちによる猿舞（猿っ子踊り）が披露される<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf 伊豆地域郷土芸能] - ACTJT</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* {{jalink|積羽八重事代主命}}

=== 境内 ===
* 忠魂碑
* 忘れじの碑 - 元{{jalink|予科練}}生（海軍少年飛行兵）が、当時の住民の親切に感謝して{{jalink|1975年|Q2479}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}50年）に建立<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto 忘れじの碑] - 西伊豆観光協会</ref>。

=== 猿舞（猿っ子踊り）===
由来は諸説あるが、漁に関係した祈願が込められていることは共通している<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。演目は、
# 立ち踊り
# 扇踊り
# 逆立ち踊り
# 鯨突き踊り
の4つ<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。

元来は青年が1人で踊っていたが、地区の青年団が無くなって後、{{jalink|1980年|Q2439}}（昭和55年）に踊りが復活してからは、小学生が集団で演じるようになった<ref name="act-jt"/>。

'''多爾夜神社'''（たにやじんじゃ）<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|西伊豆町|Q1203949}}安良里（あらり）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|Q11380552|伊豆国の式内社}}の1つである。

=== 概要 ===
創建不詳だが、{{jalink|式内社}}なので『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』が作られた{{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{jalink|コトバンク|Q11302260}}</ref>。

社名は「谷家」が語源とされ、同地区の山道を上がった先にある山神社が元宮とされる。

11月3日の例大祭では、同地区の出崎神社と共に、赤装束の子供たちによる猿舞（猿っ子踊り）が披露される<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf 伊豆地域郷土芸能] - ACTJT</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* {{jalink|積羽八重事代主命}}

=== 境内 ===
* 忠魂碑
* 忘れじの碑 - 元{{jalink|予科練}}生（海軍少年飛行兵）が、当時の住民の親切に感謝して{{jalink|1975年|Q2479}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}50年）に建立<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto 忘れじの碑] - 西伊豆観光協会</ref>。

=== 猿舞（猿っ子踊り）===
由来は諸説あるが、漁に関係した祈願が込められていることは共通している<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。演目は、
# 立ち踊り
# 扇踊り
# 逆立ち踊り
# 鯨突き踊り
の4つ<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。

元来は青年が1人で踊っていたが、地区の青年団が無くなって後、{{jalink|1980年|Q2439}}（昭和55年）に踊りが復活してからは、小学生が集団で演じるようになった<ref name="act-jt"/>。

'''多爾夜神社'''（たにやじんじゃ）<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|西伊豆町|Q1203949}}安良里（あらり）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|Q11380552|伊豆国の式内社}}の1つである。

=== 概要 ===
創建不詳だが、{{jalink|式内社}}なので『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』が作られた{{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{jalink|コトバンク|Q11302260}}</ref>。

社名は「谷家」が語源とされ、同地区の山道を上がった先にある山神社が元宮とされる。

11月3日の例大祭では、同地区の出崎神社と共に、赤装束の子供たちによる猿舞（猿っ子踊り）が披露される<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf 伊豆地域郷土芸能] - ACTJT</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* {{jalink|積羽八重事代主命}}

=== 境内 ===
* 忠魂碑
* 忘れじの碑 - 元{{jalink|予科練}}生（海軍少年飛行兵）が、当時の住民の親切に感謝して{{jalink|1975年|Q2479}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}50年）に建立<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto 忘れじの碑] - 西伊豆観光協会</ref>。

=== 猿舞（猿っ子踊り）===
由来は諸説あるが、漁に関係した祈願が込められていることは共通している<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。演目は、
# 立ち踊り
# 扇踊り
# 逆立ち踊り
# 鯨突き踊り
の4つ<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。

元来は青年が1人で踊っていたが、地区の青年団が無くなって後、{{jalink|1980年|Q2439}}（昭和55年）に踊りが復活してからは、小学生が集団で演じるようになった<ref name="act-jt"/>。

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''多爾夜神社'''（たにやじんじゃ）<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|西伊豆町|Q1203949}}安良里（あらり）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|Q11380552|伊豆国の式内社}}の1つである。

=== 概要 ===
創建不詳だが、{{jalink|式内社}}なので『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』が作られた{{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{jalink|コトバンク|Q11302260}}</ref>。

社名は「谷家」が語源とされ、同地区の山道を上がった先にある山神社が元宮とされる。

11月3日の例大祭では、同地区の出崎神社と共に、赤装束の子供たちによる猿舞（猿っ子踊り）が披露される<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf 伊豆地域郷土芸能] - ACTJT</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* {{jalink|積羽八重事代主命}}

=== 境内 ===
* 忠魂碑
* 忘れじの碑 - 元{{jalink|予科練}}生（海軍少年飛行兵）が、当時の住民の親切に感謝して{{jalink|1975年|Q2479}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}50年）に建立<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto 忘れじの碑] - 西伊豆観光協会</ref>。

=== 猿舞（猿っ子踊り）===
由来は諸説あるが、漁に関係した祈願が込められていることは共通している<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。演目は、
# 立ち踊り
# 扇踊り
# 逆立ち踊り
# 鯨突き踊り
の4つ<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。

元来は青年が1人で踊っていたが、地区の青年団が無くなって後、{{jalink|1980年|Q2439}}（昭和55年）に踊りが復活してからは、小学生が集団で演じるようになった<ref name="act-jt"/>。

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''多爾夜神社'''（たにやじんじゃ）<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|西伊豆町|Q1203949}}安良里（あらり）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|Q11380552|伊豆国の式内社}}の1つである。

=== 概要 ===
創建不詳だが、{{jalink|式内社}}なので『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』が作られた{{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{jalink|コトバンク|Q11302260}}</ref>。

社名は「谷家」が語源とされ、同地区の山道を上がった先にある山神社が元宮とされる。

11月3日の例大祭では、同地区の出崎神社と共に、赤装束の子供たちによる猿舞（猿っ子踊り）が披露される<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf 伊豆地域郷土芸能] - ACTJT</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* {{jalink|積羽八重事代主命}}

=== 境内 ===
* 忠魂碑
* 忘れじの碑 - 元{{jalink|予科練}}生（海軍少年飛行兵）が、当時の住民の親切に感謝して{{jalink|1975年|Q2479}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}50年）に建立<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto 忘れじの碑] - 西伊豆観光協会</ref>。

=== 猿舞（猿っ子踊り）===
由来は諸説あるが、漁に関係した祈願が込められていることは共通している<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。演目は、
# 立ち踊り
# 扇踊り
# 逆立ち踊り
# 鯨突き踊り
の4つ<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。

元来は青年が1人で踊っていたが、地区の青年団が無くなって後、{{jalink|1980年|Q2439}}（昭和55年）に踊りが復活してからは、小学生が集団で演じるようになった<ref name="act-jt"/>。

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''多爾夜神社'''（たにやじんじゃ）<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|西伊豆町|Q1203949}}安良里（あらり）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|Q11380552|伊豆国の式内社}}の1つである。

=== 概要 ===
創建不詳だが、{{jalink|式内社}}なので『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』が作られた{{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{jalink|コトバンク|Q11302260}}</ref>。

社名は「谷家」が語源とされ、同地区の山道を上がった先にある山神社が元宮とされる。

11月3日の例大祭では、同地区の出崎神社と共に、赤装束の子供たちによる猿舞（猿っ子踊り）が披露される<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf 伊豆地域郷土芸能] - ACTJT</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* {{jalink|積羽八重事代主命}}

=== 境内 ===
* 忠魂碑
* 忘れじの碑 - 元{{jalink|予科練}}生（海軍少年飛行兵）が、当時の住民の親切に感謝して{{jalink|1975年|Q2479}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}50年）に建立<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto 忘れじの碑] - 西伊豆観光協会</ref>。

=== 猿舞（猿っ子踊り）===
由来は諸説あるが、漁に関係した祈願が込められていることは共通している<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。演目は、
# 立ち踊り
# 扇踊り
# 逆立ち踊り
# 鯨突き踊り
の4つ<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>。

元来は青年が1人で踊っていたが、地区の青年団が無くなって後、{{jalink|1980年|Q2439}}（昭和55年）に踊りが復活してからは、小学生が集団で演じるようになった<ref name="act-jt"/>。

{{nihongo|'''Taniya Shrine'''|多爾夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Taniya Shrine''' (たにやじんじゃ)<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Arari, {{jalink|Nishiizu|Q1203949}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. It is one of the {{jalink|Shikinaisha of Izu Province|Q11380552}}.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} it dates back to before {{jalink|Enchō (era)|Q1327274|Enchō}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), when the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{jalink|Kotobank|Q11302260}}</ref>.

The shrine name is said to derive from “Taniya,” and Yama Shrine up the mountain path in the same district is said to be the original shrine.

At the annual festival on November 3, together with nearby Dezaki Shrine, children in red costumes perform the Sarumai (Sarukko dance)<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf Izu regional folk performing arts] - ACTJT</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}

=== Precincts ===
* Memorial monument to the war dead
* Wasureji-no-hi monument - erected in {{jalink|1975|Q2479}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 50) by former {{jalink|Yokaren}} trainees (naval youth aviators) in gratitude for local residents’ kindness<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto Wasureji Monument] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref>.

=== Sarumai (Sarukko dance) ===
There are several origin theories, but all share that it contains prayers related to fishing<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>. The program consists of:
# Standing dance
# Fan dance
# Handstand dance
# Whale-spearing dance
four performances in total<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>.

Originally danced solo by young men, but after local youth groups disappeared, when the dance was revived in {{jalink|1980|Q2439}} (Shōwa 55), elementary school children began performing it as a group<ref name="act-jt"/>.

'''Taniya Shrine''' (たにやじんじゃ)<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Arari, {{jalink|Nishiizu|Q1203949}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. It is one of the {{jalink|Shikinaisha of Izu Province|Q11380552}}.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} it dates back to before {{jalink|Enchō (era)|Q1327274|Enchō}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), when the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{jalink|Kotobank|Q11302260}}</ref>.

The shrine name is said to derive from “Taniya,” and Yama Shrine up the mountain path in the same district is said to be the original shrine.

At the annual festival on November 3, together with nearby Dezaki Shrine, children in red costumes perform the Sarumai (Sarukko dance)<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf Izu regional folk performing arts] - ACTJT</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}

=== Precincts ===
* Memorial monument to the war dead
* Wasureji-no-hi monument - erected in {{jalink|1975|Q2479}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 50) by former {{jalink|Yokaren}} trainees (naval youth aviators) in gratitude for local residents’ kindness<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto Wasureji Monument] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref>.

=== Sarumai (Sarukko dance) ===
There are several origin theories, but all share that it contains prayers related to fishing<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>. The program consists of:
# Standing dance
# Fan dance
# Handstand dance
# Whale-spearing dance
four performances in total<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>.

Originally danced solo by young men, but after local youth groups disappeared, when the dance was revived in {{jalink|1980|Q2439}} (Shōwa 55), elementary school children began performing it as a group<ref name="act-jt"/>.

'''Taniya Shrine''' (たにやじんじゃ)<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Arari, {{jalink|Nishiizu|Q1203949}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. It is one of the {{jalink|Shikinaisha of Izu Province|Q11380552}}.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} it dates back to before {{jalink|Enchō (era)|Q1327274|Enchō}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), when the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{jalink|Kotobank|Q11302260}}</ref>.

The shrine name is said to derive from “Taniya,” and Yama Shrine up the mountain path in the same district is said to be the original shrine.

At the annual festival on November 3, together with nearby Dezaki Shrine, children in red costumes perform the Sarumai (Sarukko dance)<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf Izu regional folk performing arts] - ACTJT</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}

=== Precincts ===
* Memorial monument to the war dead
* Wasureji-no-hi monument - erected in {{jalink|1975|Q2479}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 50) by former {{jalink|Yokaren}} trainees (naval youth aviators) in gratitude for local residents’ kindness<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto Wasureji Monument] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref>.

=== Sarumai (Sarukko dance) ===
There are several origin theories, but all share that it contains prayers related to fishing<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>. The program consists of:
# Standing dance
# Fan dance
# Handstand dance
# Whale-spearing dance
four performances in total<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>.

Originally danced solo by young men, but after local youth groups disappeared, when the dance was revived in {{jalink|1980|Q2439}} (Shōwa 55), elementary school children began performing it as a group<ref name="act-jt"/>.

=== Notes ===

'''Taniya Shrine''' (たにやじんじゃ)<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Arari, {{jalink|Nishiizu|Q1203949}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. It is one of the {{jalink|Shikinaisha of Izu Province|Q11380552}}.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} it dates back to before {{jalink|Enchō (era)|Q1327274|Enchō}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), when the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{jalink|Kotobank|Q11302260}}</ref>.

The shrine name is said to derive from “Taniya,” and Yama Shrine up the mountain path in the same district is said to be the original shrine.

At the annual festival on November 3, together with nearby Dezaki Shrine, children in red costumes perform the Sarumai (Sarukko dance)<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf Izu regional folk performing arts] - ACTJT</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}

=== Precincts ===
* Memorial monument to the war dead
* Wasureji-no-hi monument - erected in {{jalink|1975|Q2479}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 50) by former {{jalink|Yokaren}} trainees (naval youth aviators) in gratitude for local residents’ kindness<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto Wasureji Monument] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref>.

=== Sarumai (Sarukko dance) ===
There are several origin theories, but all share that it contains prayers related to fishing<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>. The program consists of:
# Standing dance
# Fan dance
# Handstand dance
# Whale-spearing dance
four performances in total<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>.

Originally danced solo by young men, but after local youth groups disappeared, when the dance was revived in {{jalink|1980|Q2439}} (Shōwa 55), elementary school children began performing it as a group<ref name="act-jt"/>.

'''Taniya Shrine''' (たにやじんじゃ)<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Arari, {{jalink|Nishiizu|Q1203949}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. It is one of the {{jalink|Shikinaisha of Izu Province|Q11380552}}.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} it dates back to before {{jalink|Enchō (era)|Q1327274|Enchō}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), when the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{jalink|Kotobank|Q11302260}}</ref>.

The shrine name is said to derive from “Taniya,” and Yama Shrine up the mountain path in the same district is said to be the original shrine.

At the annual festival on November 3, together with nearby Dezaki Shrine, children in red costumes perform the Sarumai (Sarukko dance)<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf Izu regional folk performing arts] - ACTJT</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}

=== Precincts ===
* Memorial monument to the war dead
* Wasureji-no-hi monument - erected in {{jalink|1975|Q2479}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 50) by former {{jalink|Yokaren}} trainees (naval youth aviators) in gratitude for local residents’ kindness<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto Wasureji Monument] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref>.

=== Sarumai (Sarukko dance) ===
There are several origin theories, but all share that it contains prayers related to fishing<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>. The program consists of:
# Standing dance
# Fan dance
# Handstand dance
# Whale-spearing dance
four performances in total<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>.

Originally danced solo by young men, but after local youth groups disappeared, when the dance was revived in {{jalink|1980|Q2439}} (Shōwa 55), elementary school children began performing it as a group<ref name="act-jt"/>.

'''Taniya Shrine''' (たにやじんじゃ)<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Arari, {{jalink|Nishiizu|Q1203949}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. It is one of the {{jalink|Shikinaisha of Izu Province|Q11380552}}.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} it dates back to before {{jalink|Enchō (era)|Q1327274|Enchō}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), when the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{jalink|Kotobank|Q11302260}}</ref>.

The shrine name is said to derive from “Taniya,” and Yama Shrine up the mountain path in the same district is said to be the original shrine.

At the annual festival on November 3, together with nearby Dezaki Shrine, children in red costumes perform the Sarumai (Sarukko dance)<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf Izu regional folk performing arts] - ACTJT</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}

=== Precincts ===
* Memorial monument to the war dead
* Wasureji-no-hi monument - erected in {{jalink|1975|Q2479}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 50) by former {{jalink|Yokaren}} trainees (naval youth aviators) in gratitude for local residents’ kindness<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto Wasureji Monument] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref>.

=== Sarumai (Sarukko dance) ===
There are several origin theories, but all share that it contains prayers related to fishing<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>. The program consists of:
# Standing dance
# Fan dance
# Handstand dance
# Whale-spearing dance
four performances in total<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>.

Originally danced solo by young men, but after local youth groups disappeared, when the dance was revived in {{jalink|1980|Q2439}} (Shōwa 55), elementary school children began performing it as a group<ref name="act-jt"/>.

'''Taniya Shrine''' (たにやじんじゃ)<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Arari, {{jalink|Nishiizu|Q1203949}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. It is one of the {{jalink|Shikinaisha of Izu Province|Q11380552}}.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} it dates back to before {{jalink|Enchō (era)|Q1327274|Enchō}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), when the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{jalink|Kotobank|Q11302260}}</ref>.

The shrine name is said to derive from “Taniya,” and Yama Shrine up the mountain path in the same district is said to be the original shrine.

At the annual festival on November 3, together with nearby Dezaki Shrine, children in red costumes perform the Sarumai (Sarukko dance)<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf Izu regional folk performing arts] - ACTJT</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}

=== Precincts ===
* Memorial monument to the war dead
* Wasureji-no-hi monument - erected in {{jalink|1975|Q2479}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 50) by former {{jalink|Yokaren}} trainees (naval youth aviators) in gratitude for local residents’ kindness<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto Wasureji Monument] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref>.

=== Sarumai (Sarukko dance) ===
There are several origin theories, but all share that it contains prayers related to fishing<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>. The program consists of:
# Standing dance
# Fan dance
# Handstand dance
# Whale-spearing dance
four performances in total<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>.

Originally danced solo by young men, but after local youth groups disappeared, when the dance was revived in {{jalink|1980|Q2439}} (Shōwa 55), elementary school children began performing it as a group<ref name="act-jt"/>.

'''Taniya Shrine''' (たにやじんじゃ)<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Arari, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552}}.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but as a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260}}</ref>.

The shrine name is said to derive from “Taniya,” and Yama Shrine up the mountain path in the same district is said to be the original shrine.

At the annual festival on November 3, together with nearby Dezaki Shrine, children in red costumes perform the Sarumai (Sarukko dance)<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf Izu regional folk performing arts] - ACTJT</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill||lt=Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}

=== Precincts ===
* Memorial monument to the war dead
* Wasureji-no-hi monument - erected in {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 50) by former {{ill||lt=Yokaren|UNKNOWN}} trainees (naval youth aviators) in gratitude for local residents’ kindness<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto Wasureji Monument] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref>.

=== Sarumai (Sarukko dance) ===
There are several origin theories, but all share that it contains prayers related to fishing<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>. The program consists of:
# Standing dance
# Fan dance
# Handstand dance
# Whale-spearing dance
four performances in total<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>.

Originally danced solo by young men, but after local youth groups disappeared, when the dance was revived in {{ill|lt=1980|Q2439|WD=Q2439}} (Shōwa 55), elementary school children began performing it as a group<ref name="act-jt"/>.

'''Taniya Shrine''' (たにやじんじゃ)<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Arari, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552}}.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260}}</ref>.

The shrine name is said to derive from “Taniya,” and Yama Shrine up the mountain path in the same district is said to be the original shrine.

At the annual festival on November 3, together with nearby Dezaki Shrine, children in red costumes perform the Sarumai (Sarukko dance)<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf Izu regional folk performing arts] - ACTJT</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}

=== Precincts ===
* Memorial monument to the war dead
* Wasureji-no-hi monument - erected in {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 50) by former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yokaren|UNKNOWN}} trainees (naval youth aviators) in gratitude for local residents’ kindness<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto Wasureji Monument] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref>.

=== Sarumai (Sarukko dance) ===
There are several origin theories, but all share that it contains prayers related to fishing<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>. The program consists of:
# Standing dance
# Fan dance
# Handstand dance
# Whale-spearing dance
four performances in total<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>.

Originally danced solo by young men, but after local youth groups disappeared, when the dance was revived in {{ill|lt=1980|Q2439|WD=Q2439}} (Shōwa 55), elementary school children began performing it as a group<ref name="act-jt"/>.

'''Taniya Shrine''' (たにやじんじゃ)<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Arari, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552}}.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260}}</ref>.

The shrine name is said to derive from “Taniya,” and Yama Shrine up the mountain path in the same district is said to be the original shrine.

At the annual festival on November 3, together with nearby Dezaki Shrine, children in red costumes perform the Sarumai (Sarukko dance)<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf Izu regional folk performing arts] - ACTJT</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}

=== Precincts ===
* Memorial monument to the war dead
* Wasureji-no-hi monument - erected in {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 50) by former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yokaren|1=UNKNOWN}} trainees (naval youth aviators) in gratitude for local residents’ kindness<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto Wasureji Monument] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref>.

=== Sarumai (Sarukko dance) ===
There are several origin theories, but all share that it contains prayers related to fishing<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>. The program consists of:
# Standing dance
# Fan dance
# Handstand dance
# Whale-spearing dance
four performances in total<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>.

Originally danced solo by young men, but after local youth groups disappeared, when the dance was revived in {{ill|lt=1980|Q2439|WD=Q2439}} (Shōwa 55), elementary school children began performing it as a group<ref name="act-jt"/>.

'''Taniya Shrine''' (たにやじんじゃ)<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Arari, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552}}.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260}}</ref>.

The shrine name is said to derive from “Taniya,” and Yama Shrine up the mountain path in the same district is said to be the original shrine.

At the annual festival on November 3, together with nearby Dezaki Shrine, children in red costumes perform the Sarumai (Sarukko dance)<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf Izu regional folk performing arts] - ACTJT</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}

=== Precincts ===
* Memorial monument to the war dead
* Wasureji-no-hi monument - erected in {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 50) by former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yokaren|1=UNKNOWN}} trainees (naval youth aviators) in gratitude for local residents’ kindness<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto Wasureji Monument] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref>.

=== Sarumai (Sarukko dance) ===
There are several origin theories, but all share that it contains prayers related to fishing<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>. The program consists of:
# Standing dance
# Fan dance
# Handstand dance
# Whale-spearing dance
four performances in total<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>.

Originally danced solo by young men, but after local youth groups disappeared, when the dance was revived in {{ill|lt=1980|Q2439|WD=Q2439}} (Shōwa 55), elementary school children began performing it as a group<ref name="act-jt"/>.

'''Taniya Shrine''' (たにやじんじゃ)<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Arari, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552}}.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260}}</ref>.

The shrine name is said to derive from “Taniya,” and Yama Shrine up the mountain path in the same district is said to be the original shrine.

At the annual festival on November 3, together with nearby Dezaki Shrine, children in red costumes perform the Sarumai (Sarukko dance)<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf Izu regional folk performing arts] - ACTJT</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}

=== Precincts ===
* Memorial monument to the war dead
* Wasureji-no-hi monument - erected in {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 50) by former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yokaren|1=UNKNOWN}} trainees (naval youth aviators) in gratitude for local residents’ kindness<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto Wasureji Monument] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref>.

=== Sarumai (Sarukko dance) ===
There are several origin theories, but all share that it contains prayers related to fishing<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>. The program consists of:
# Standing dance
# Fan dance
# Handstand dance
# Whale-spearing dance
four performances in total<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>.

Originally danced solo by young men, but after local youth groups disappeared, when the dance was revived in {{ill|lt=1980|Q2439|WD=Q2439}} (Shōwa 55), elementary school children began performing it as a group<ref name="act-jt"/>.

'''Taniya Shrine''' (たにやじんじゃ)<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Arari, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552}}.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260}}</ref>.

The shrine name is said to derive from “Taniya,” and Yama Shrine up the mountain path in the same district is said to be the original shrine.

At the annual festival on November 3, together with nearby Dezaki Shrine, children in red costumes perform the Sarumai (Sarukko dance)<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf Izu regional folk performing arts] - ACTJT</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}

=== Precincts ===
* Memorial monument to the war dead
* Wasureji-no-hi monument - erected in {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 50) by former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yokaren|1=UNKNOWN}} trainees (naval youth aviators) in gratitude for local residents’ kindness<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto Wasureji Monument] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref>.

=== Sarumai (Sarukko dance) ===
There are several origin theories, but all share that it contains prayers related to fishing<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>. The program consists of:
# Standing dance
# Fan dance
# Handstand dance
# Whale-spearing dance
four performances in total<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>.

Originally danced solo by young men, but after local youth groups disappeared, when the dance was revived in {{ill|lt=1980|Q2439|WD=Q2439}} (Shōwa 55), elementary school children began performing it as a group<ref name="act-jt"/>.

'''Taniya Shrine''' (たにやじんじゃ)<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Arari, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949|1=Nishiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260|1=Kotobank}}</ref>.

The shrine name is said to derive from “Taniya,” and Yama Shrine up the mountain path in the same district is said to be the original shrine.

At the annual festival on November 3, together with nearby Dezaki Shrine, children in red costumes perform the Sarumai (Sarukko dance)<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf Izu regional folk performing arts] - ACTJT</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}

=== Precincts ===
* Memorial monument to the war dead
* Wasureji-no-hi monument - erected in {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479|1=1975}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 50) by former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yokaren|1=UNKNOWN}} trainees (naval youth aviators) in gratitude for local residents’ kindness<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto Wasureji Monument] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref>.

=== Sarumai (Sarukko dance) ===
There are several origin theories, but all share that it contains prayers related to fishing<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>. The program consists of:
# Standing dance
# Fan dance
# Handstand dance
# Whale-spearing dance
four performances in total<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>.

Originally danced solo by young men, but after local youth groups disappeared, when the dance was revived in {{ill|lt=1980|Q2439|WD=Q2439|1=1980}} (Shōwa 55), elementary school children began performing it as a group<ref name="act-jt"/>.

'''Taniya Shrine''' (たにやじんじゃ)<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Arari, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949|1=Nishiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260|1=Kotobank}}</ref>.

The shrine name is said to derive from “Taniya,” and Yama Shrine up the mountain path in the same district is said to be the original shrine.

At the annual festival on November 3, together with nearby Dezaki Shrine, children in red costumes perform the Sarumai (Sarukko dance)<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf Izu regional folk performing arts] - ACTJT</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}

=== Precincts ===
* Memorial monument to the war dead
* Wasureji-no-hi monument - erected in {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479|1=1975}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 50) by former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yokaren|1=UNKNOWN}} trainees (naval youth aviators) in gratitude for local residents’ kindness<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto Wasureji Monument] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref>.

=== Sarumai (Sarukko dance) ===
There are several origin theories, but all share that it contains prayers related to fishing<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>. The program consists of:
# Standing dance
# Fan dance
# Handstand dance
# Whale-spearing dance
four performances in total<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>.

Originally danced solo by young men, but after local youth groups disappeared, when the dance was revived in {{ill|lt=1980|Q2439|WD=Q2439|1=1980}} (Shōwa 55), elementary school children began performing it as a group<ref name="act-jt"/>.

'''Taniya Shrine''' (たにやじんじゃ)<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Arari, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949|1=Nishiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260|1=Kotobank}}</ref>.

The shrine name is said to derive from “Taniya,” and Yama Shrine up the mountain path in the same district is said to be the original shrine.

At the annual festival on November 3, together with nearby Dezaki Shrine, children in red costumes perform the Sarumai (Sarukko dance)<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf Izu regional folk performing arts] - ACTJT</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}

=== Precincts ===
* Memorial monument to the war dead
* Wasureji-no-hi monument - erected in {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479|1=1975}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 50) by former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yokaren|1=UNKNOWN}} trainees (naval youth aviators) in gratitude for local residents’ kindness<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto Wasureji Monument] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref>.

=== Sarumai (Sarukko dance) ===
There are several origin theories, but all share that it contains prayers related to fishing<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>. The program consists of:
# Standing dance
# Fan dance
# Handstand dance
# Whale-spearing dance
four performances in total<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>.

Originally danced solo by young men, but after local youth groups disappeared, when the dance was revived in {{ill|lt=1980|Q2439|WD=Q2439|1=1980}} (Shōwa 55), elementary school children began performing it as a group<ref name="act-jt"/>.

'''Taniya Shrine''' (たにやじんじゃ)<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Arari, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949|1=Nishiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260|1=Kotobank}}</ref>.

The shrine name is said to derive from “Taniya,” and Yama Shrine up the mountain path in the same district is said to be the original shrine.

At the annual festival on November 3, together with nearby Dezaki Shrine, children in red costumes perform the Sarumai (Sarukko dance)<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf Izu regional folk performing arts] - ACTJT</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}

=== Precincts ===
* Memorial monument to the war dead
* Wasureji-no-hi monument - erected in {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479|1=1975}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 50) by former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yokaren|1=UNKNOWN}} trainees (naval youth aviators) in gratitude for local residents’ kindness<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto Wasureji Monument] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref>.

=== Sarumai (Sarukko dance) ===
There are several origin theories, but all share that it contains prayers related to fishing<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>. The program consists of:
# Standing dance
# Fan dance
# Handstand dance
# Whale-spearing dance
four performances in total<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>.

Originally danced solo by young men, but after local youth groups disappeared, when the dance was revived in {{ill|lt=1980|Q2439|WD=Q2439|1=1980}} (Shōwa 55), elementary school children began performing it as a group<ref name="act-jt"/>.

'''Taniya Shrine''' (たにやじんじゃ)<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Arari, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949|1=Nishiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260|1=Kotobank}}</ref>.

The shrine name is said to derive from “Taniya,” and Yama Shrine up the mountain path in the same district is said to be the original shrine.

At the annual festival on November 3, together with nearby Dezaki Shrine, children in red costumes perform the Sarumai (Sarukko dance)<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf Izu regional folk performing arts] - ACTJT</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}

=== Precincts ===
* Memorial monument to the war dead
* Wasureji-no-hi monument - erected in {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479|1=1975}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 50) by former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yokaren|1=UNKNOWN}} trainees (naval youth aviators) in gratitude for local residents’ kindness<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto Wasureji Monument] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref>.

=== Sarumai (Sarukko dance) ===
There are several origin theories, but all share that it contains prayers related to fishing<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>. The program consists of:
# Standing dance
# Fan dance
# Handstand dance
# Whale-spearing dance
four performances in total<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>.

Originally danced solo by young men, but after local youth groups disappeared, when the dance was revived in {{ill|lt=1980|Q2439|WD=Q2439|1=1980}} (Shōwa 55), elementary school children began performing it as a group<ref name="act-jt"/>.

'''Taniya Shrine''' (たにやじんじゃ)<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Arari, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949|1=Nishiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260|1=Kotobank}}</ref>.

The shrine name is said to derive from “Taniya,” and Yama Shrine up the mountain path in the same district is said to be the original shrine.

At the annual festival on November 3, together with nearby Dezaki Shrine, children in red costumes perform the Sarumai (Sarukko dance)<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf Izu regional folk performing arts] - ACTJT</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}

=== Precincts ===
* Memorial monument to the war dead
* Wasureji-no-hi monument - erected in {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479|1=1975}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 50) by former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yokaren|1=UNKNOWN}} trainees (naval youth aviators) in gratitude for local residents’ kindness<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto Wasureji Monument] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref>.

=== Sarumai (Sarukko dance) ===
There are several origin theories, but all share that it contains prayers related to fishing<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>. The program consists of:
# Standing dance
# Fan dance
# Handstand dance
# Whale-spearing dance
four performances in total<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>.

Originally danced solo by young men, but after local youth groups disappeared, when the dance was revived in {{ill|lt=1980|Q2439|WD=Q2439|1=1980}} (Shōwa 55), elementary school children began performing it as a group<ref name="act-jt"/>.

'''Taniya Shrine''' (たにやじんじゃ)<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Arari, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949|1=Nishiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260|1=Kotobank}}</ref>.

The shrine name is said to derive from “Taniya,” and Yama Shrine up the mountain path in the same district is said to be the original shrine.

At the annual festival on November 3, together with nearby Dezaki Shrine, children in red costumes perform the Sarumai (Sarukko dance)<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf Izu regional folk performing arts] - ACTJT</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}

=== Precincts ===
* Memorial monument to the war dead
* Wasureji-no-hi monument - erected in {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479|1=1975}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 50) by former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yokaren|1=UNKNOWN}} trainees (naval youth aviators) in gratitude for local residents’ kindness<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto Wasureji Monument] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref>.

=== Sarumai (Sarukko dance) ===
There are several origin theories, but all share that it contains prayers related to fishing<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>. The program consists of:
# Standing dance
# Fan dance
# Handstand dance
# Whale-spearing dance
four performances in total<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>.

Originally danced solo by young men, but after local youth groups disappeared, when the dance was revived in {{ill|lt=1980|Q2439|WD=Q2439|1=1980}} (Shōwa 55), elementary school children began performing it as a group<ref name="act-jt"/>.

'''Taniya Shrine''' (たにやじんじゃ)<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401180 多爾夜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Arari, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949|1=Nishiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260|1=Kotobank}}</ref>.

The shrine name is said to derive from “Taniya,” and Yama Shrine up the mountain path in the same district is said to be the original shrine.

At the annual festival on November 3, together with nearby Dezaki Shrine, children in red costumes perform the Sarumai (Sarukko dance)<ref name="kk">[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/taniyasarumai 多爾夜神社猿舞] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref name="act-jt">[http://odoriko.act-jt.jp/report.pdf Izu regional folk performing arts] - ACTJT</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}

=== Precincts ===
* Memorial monument to the war dead
* Wasureji-no-hi monument - erected in {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479|1=1975}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 50) by former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yokaren|1=UNKNOWN}} trainees (naval youth aviators) in gratitude for local residents’ kindness<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/wasurejinoto Wasureji Monument] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref>.

=== Sarumai (Sarukko dance) ===
There are several origin theories, but all share that it contains prayers related to fishing<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>. The program consists of:
# Standing dance
# Fan dance
# Handstand dance
# Whale-spearing dance
four performances in total<ref name="kk"/><ref name="act-jt"/>.

Originally danced solo by young men, but after local youth groups disappeared, when the dance was revived in {{ill|lt=1980|Q2439|WD=Q2439|1=1980}} (Shōwa 55), elementary school children began performing it as a group<ref name="act-jt"/>.
